mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding output capacity and rising corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO growth on 60-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or Sharpe ratio of triggered long positions is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals imminent consumer weakness and recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations collapse.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE exceeds 5%, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated and the federal funds rate is simultaneously high, the combined pressure from eroding real earnings and restrictive policy causes equity valuations to contract sharply over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL*FEDFUNDS) has a positive coefficient in a regression of 20-day forward returns, or the hit rate of negative returns in triggered periods is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts in the early phase of an economic recovery indicate durable demand growth and construction-led job creation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the recovery deepens.
failure_condition: In periods following a recession (GDPC1 trough), the Rank IC of HOUST on 60-day returns is below 0.05 or the mean return is not positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1% signals a sudden drop in consumer spending power, pushing S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days as retail and discretionary sectors underperform.
failure_condition: After a 1%+ MoM drop in RSAFS, the median 20-day forward SPX return is positive or the probability of a negative return is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising employment and industrial production indicate synchronized economic strength across labor and output markets, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal (e.g., sum of standardized growth rates) has a negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year walk-forward period, or the long-only strategy based on the signal underperforms the SPX buy-and-hold.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, further increases in industrial production signal overheating that forces the Fed to tighten more aggressively, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5 and INDPRO is rising, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the negative return condition fails in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in monthly inflation, combined with an already elevated federal funds rate, triggers immediate repricing of equity risk premia downward, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After a CPIAUCSL MoM print above 0.4% when FEDFUNDS is above 4%, the average 5-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 50%.
